description: Born in Kronach as son of a painter.
Possible stay in Nuremberg (in the workshop of Michael Wolgemut or Albrecht Dürer?).
Work in Vienna in the circle of humanists around Conrad Celtis.
Appointment as court painter to Wittenberg by the Saxon elector Friedrich III, the Wise.
Award of a heraldic letter by Frederick III in Nuremberg: crowned snake with bad wings and a ruby ring in its mouth.
Stay at the court of Margaret of Austria, governor of the Netherlands, in Mechelen.
Acquisition of two neighboring houses on the Wittenberg market (today Nos. 3 and 4), followed by the relocation of the workshop from the castle to the city.
Wedding with Barbara Brengbier and birth of her son Hans.
Birth of the son Lucas Cranach the Younger.
Birth of daughter Ursula.
Acquisition of the 'Cranachhof', Schlossstraße No. 1 in Wittenberg.
Acceptance of the office as council member in Wittenberg.
Preservation of the pharmacist privilege, Birth of daughter Barbara.
Reception of King Christian II of Denmark as guest in his house.
Running a printing office with Christian Döring (until 1525/1526).
Accompaniment of Friedrich III, the Wise to the Reichstag in Nuremberg; there he met Albrecht Dürer.
Best man at the marriage of Martin Luther and Katharina von Bora.
Work for the new Elector Johann the Steadfast.
Birth of daughter Anna.
According to tax records, Cranach was the richest citizen of Wittenberg after the Saxon chancellor Brück, whose son would marry Cranach's daughter Barbara in 1541.
Works for the new Elector Johann Friedrich I, called the Magnanimous
Taking office as mayor of Wittenberg.
Temporary loss of his employment as court painter (until 1550).
Stay in Augsburg and Innsbruck as court painter of the imprisoned Johann Friedrich I.; meeting with Titian.
Start of his work in Weimar.
Lucas Cranach the Elder dies on October 16 in Weimar.
